name: Local History Lost Electric Sign Example
description: A fictionalized-but-source-grounded local history example pack demonstrating historical evidence preservation: a 1927 newspaper clipping, its transcription, structured claims, people/organizations, a timeline, research leads, and open questions about a small-town electric sign whose ultimate fate is unknown.

usage_policy.notes: This example is intended for historical evidence packaging, retrieval, and evaluation demonstrations. The presence of a source clipping and transcription does not prove all historical claims beyond the cited source. The pack preserves unresolved questions and research leads rather than claiming a complete history.

artifacts.title: Newspaper clipping — The Pomona Progress, June 29, 1927, page 5
artifacts.description: Scanned image of the original newspaper clipping reporting the chamber of commerce's decision on the electric sign.

artifacts.title: Plain-text transcription of the newspaper clipping
artifacts.description: Manual plain-text transcription of the clipping image, used as the raw source text for records in this pack.

artifacts.title: Article Transcription
artifacts.description: Presented transcription of the article with source citation and transcription-method notes.

artifacts.title: Research Summary
artifacts.description: Summary of what is source-attested versus unknown, and what this pack does not claim.

artifacts.title: Location Analysis
artifacts.description: Analysis of the reported location and the unresolved question of the sign's final orientation.

artifacts.title: Unanswered Questions
artifacts.description: The open research questions this pack's source does not answer.

artifacts.title: Source excerpt records
artifacts.description: Structured OKPF records for the newspaper source excerpt.

artifacts.title: Historical claim records
artifacts.description: Structured OKPF records for each source-attested historical claim.

artifacts.title: People and organization records
artifacts.description: Structured OKPF records for people, organizations, and the source publication referenced in the article.

artifacts.title: Timeline records
artifacts.description: Structured OKPF records for the known and inferred sequence of events.

artifacts.title: Research lead records
artifacts.description: Structured OKPF records for candidate next research steps.

artifacts.title: Negative search records
artifacts.description: Placeholder structured OKPF records demonstrating how to record searches with no result, or not yet performed.

artifacts.title: Provenance sources
artifacts.description: Provenance entry describing the newspaper clipping source.

artifacts.title: Transcription notes
artifacts.description: Method, reviewer status, and known limitations of the manual transcription.

artifacts.title: Search log
artifacts.description: Placeholder search log demonstrating how future research searches should be recorded.

artifacts.title: Historical research questions
artifacts.description: Evaluation questions testing whether a consumer correctly distinguishes source-attested facts from unresolved questions.

records.description: Source excerpt records for the newspaper clipping.
Historical claim records extracted from the source excerpt.
People, organization, and publication reference records.
Timeline event records, with uncertainty marked explicitly.
Candidate research lead records for future investigation.

evals.description: Questions testing whether a consumer respects the pack's documented uncertainty.
